semantic.role: Infrastructure facade for manifest and language snapshot persistence in the ArchSpine mirror system.
responsibilities: Read and load the runtime manifest state (e.g., reverse index completeness) from the manifest file.
Determine whether an initial sync is needed based on manifest existence and the global status from a provided source.
Persist the reverse index completion flag back to the manifest file.
Save a complete manifest state including metadata (sync mode, duration, LLM information) and active violations.
Load and save the language snapshot (LanguageSnapshot) to and from disk.
Provide stable typed interfaces (ManifestRuntimeState, ManifestStatusSource) for consumers of this module.
outOfScope: Actual reverse index building or language detection logic.
Database queries for global status or active violations (provided via ManifestStatusSource).
invariants: All manifest writes go through FileSystemManager.safeWriteFile for atomicity.
Manifest file is always stored as JSON at the path returned by getManifestPath.
Language snapshot is always stored as JSON at the path returned by getLanguageSnapshotPath.
Manifest state must include schema version and generator version from the protocol types.
The module respects the infra-facade-imports rule: it only uses public facades (io.js, fs.js) and does not reach into deep private implementation paths.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a stable, isolated persistence layer for the manifest and language snapshots, enabling other parts of the system to reliably persist and query state without depending on low-level file I/O details.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Initial commit introducing the manifest and snapshot persistence module.

publicSurface.description: Interface representing the runtime state of the manifest, currently containing a reverseIndexComplete flag.
Interface for sources that can provide global status and active violation data (e.g., from a database repository).
Reads the manifest file from disk and returns the runtime state (ManifestRuntimeState).
Determines whether an initial synchronization is required, based on the existence of a manifest file and the current global status.
Updates the reverse index completion flag in the manifest file without altering other state.
Writes a complete manifest state to disk, including metadata such as sync mode, duration, LLM details, and active violations.
Reads the language snapshot file from disk and returns a LanguageSnapshot object, or null if the file does not exist.
Writes a LanguageSnapshot object to disk as a JSON file.

exports.name: saveManifestState
